AdmissionAuditRecord.description: A redacted pre-auth/session-admission denial record. This is separate from [`AuditEnvelope`] because no authenticated principal may exist yet.

correlation_id.description: Cross-realm correlation id shared across route and audit hops.

decision.description: Admission decision. Admission records are denials only.

operation_id.description: Stable audit/correlation id for this event.

principal.description: Authenticated principal, when admission failed after a principal was bound. Absent for pre-auth denials.

realm.description: Realm path for the attempted admission.

AllocatorConflict.description: Conflict metadata returned on denial. Only opaque ids and closed reasons.

AllocatorEventMetadata.description: Bounded allocator event metadata. Carries no paths, interface names, command output, or credentials.

AuditChainCheckResult.description: Verification result for one audit-chain link.

AuditChainLink.description: One link in a tamper-evident audit stream.

payloadHash.description: Hash of the canonical redacted audit payload.

previousHash.description: Previous record hash, or the stream's genesis hash for sequence 0.

recordHash.description: Hash of the canonical chain record.

AuditChainRecord.description: Redacted chain metadata for a gateway, remote-node, or daemon audit event.

AuditEnvelope.description:
A redacted post-auth audit envelope for one mutating operation or stream-open.

Construct via [`AuditEnvelope::post_auth`]. The principal is a mandatory value, not an `Option`, so an operation/stream audit event can never be recorded without an accountable authenticated principal.

correlation_id.description: Cross-realm correlation id shared across route and audit hops.

execution.description: Correlated execution, for exec audit events.

operation_id.description: Stable audit id for this event.

stream.description: Correlated stream, for stream-open audit events.

workload.description: Target workload, when the operation has one.

AuditRetentionFloorReason.description: Bounded reason for audit retention-floor status.

AuditRetentionFloorStatus.description: Retention-floor status for an audit sink.

AuditSinkHealth.description: Health state for a gateway/remote-node/daemon audit sink.

AuditSinkHealthReason.description: Bounded reason for audit sink health.

AuditStreamKind.description: Closed audit stream kinds that can carry tamper-evident hash chains.

AuthorizationScope.description: What an audited operation was authorized against. Workload operations require a [`Capability`]; node-control, identity lifecycle, and read-only health operations are authorized by node enrollment / session identity, so they have their own scope rather than a synthetic capability.

oneOf.description: Realm identity or controller-generation key rotation.

AuthzDecision.description: The authorization decision recorded for an operation/stream.

Capability.description: A named, independently-authorized capability. Display, clipboard, audio, HID, and USB are deliberately distinct so display forwarding cannot smuggle clipboard or device access.

oneOf.description: Durable execution logs with resume cursors.
One stream per connection; never a generic network bridge.
Persistent named shell operations and their shell-authorized PTY streams.
Encoded frame/video stream for environments without host Wayland.
Execute a configured launcher item. The caller supplies only the workload target and item id; runtime argv remains provider-private.

CapabilityNegotiation.description: Versioned negotiated capability set. The fingerprint is deterministic and bounded so audit records can cite the negotiated set without expanding it into every event.

CapabilityPreflightDenialReason.description: Why capability preflight denied a binding.

CapabilityPreflightStatus.description: Capability preflight status for a resolved binding.

ChildKeyPin.description: Child identity key pinned by a parent during enrollment.

ConstellationError.description: A typed realm error: a [`ErrorKind`], an optional structured missing capability (for `CapabilityDenied`), plus a bounded, operator-safe message. The message MUST NOT contain secrets, command output, store paths, argv, or stream payload, and is bounded at decode so it can never be an unbounded side channel.

capability.description: The structured missing capability, present for `CapabilityDenied` so callers route on a stable field rather than parsing the message.

correlation_id.description: Cross-realm correlation id when this error is tied to an operation.

kind.description: Stable classification. Private so the `CapabilityDenied` => `Some(capability)` invariant cannot be broken by external mutation or struct-literal construction.

negotiated_capability_fingerprint.description: Fingerprint of the negotiated capability set used for the denial.

ConstellationFrame.description: The semantic frame exchanged over a realm peer session. The codec layer maps bytes to/from this enum; the operation layer never depends on the encoding. Every variant wraps a `deny_unknown_fields` struct so a peer cannot smuggle extra fields past the decoder.

capabilities.description: Versioned positive capability assertions negotiated for this session.

codec_id.description: Codec id negotiated for this session (bounded token).

peer.description: Reserved peer-binding seam. Absent in the bootstrap protocol.

schema_fingerprint.description: Codec schema fingerprint selected for this session (bounded token).

correlation_id.description: Cross-realm correlation id shared across route and audit hops.

idempotency_key.description: Caller-generated idempotency key (required for mutating ops).

realm.description: Target realm path (most-specific-first; supports nested realms).

correlation_id.description: Cross-realm correlation id shared across response, route, and audit hops.

oneOf.description: Open a named stream (descriptor + authorization context).

authz.description: The authorization context (principal, realm, derived capability).

operation_id.description: The authorizing operation id (binds this open to one operation).

oneOf.description: A bounded chunk of stream data (opaque payload).

channel.description: Logical sub-channel within the stream. Defaults to [`StreamChannel::Primary`]; `Stdio` streams use `Stdout`/`Stderr`.

cursor.description: For a `Logs` stream, the durable resume cursor this chunk advances to. Absent for non-resumable streams.

data.description: Opaque, bounded chunk bytes. Never logged/audited as content.

sequence.description: Monotonic, per-stream sequence number (starts at 0). The receiver rejects a gap or a regression (fail-closed) rather than silently reordering.

oneOf.description: Grant additional send credit on a stream (backpressure).

credits.description: Additional frames the sender may now emit on `stream`.

reason.description: Why the stream closed (orderly completion vs cancel/timeout/error/ peer-gone). Distinguishes an expected end-of-stream from a fault without inspecting payload bytes.

oneOf.description: Resume a durable stream from a cursor.

cursor.description: Durable cursor the peer wants to resume from.

capability.description: The structured missing capability, present for `CapabilityDenied` so callers route on a stable field rather than parsing the message.

correlation_id.description: Cross-realm correlation id when this error is tied to an operation.

kind.description: Stable classification. Private so the `CapabilityDenied` => `Some(capability)` invariant cannot be broken by external mutation or struct-literal construction.

negotiated_capability_fingerprint.description: Fingerprint of the negotiated capability set used for the denial.

correlation_id.description: Cross-realm correlation id shared across route and audit hops.

decision.description: Admission decision. Admission records are denials only.

operation_id.description: Stable audit/correlation id for this event.

principal.description: Authenticated principal, when admission failed after a principal was bound. Absent for pre-auth denials.

realm.description: Realm path for the attempted admission.

ControllerGenerationMetadata.description: Metadata for a realm controller generation and its credential reference.

ControllerGenerationStatus.description: Lifecycle state of a controller-generation credential.

DefaultRealmSelectionMetadata.description: Metadata proving whether default-realm selection was involved in resolving a target.

DefaultRealmSelectionSource.description: Why a default realm was selected for a bare workload target.

oneOf.description: The process or daemon configuration selected the default.
The caller selected the default explicitly.
The resolver selected the reserved local realm for compatibility.

DescendantRoute.description: One descendant route advertised by a realm controller.

capabilities.description: Positive capabilities reachable on this route.

descendant.description: Descendant reachable below the advertising realm.

nextHopChild.description: Next child label below the advertiser.

routeId.description: Route id for this descendant path.

DirectShortcutAuthorizationMetadata.description: Direct shortcut authorization metadata. No underlay address or tunnel endpoint is represented; the path remains the authorized tree path.

DiscoveryIngressClass.description: Coarse discovery ingress class. Raw relay endpoints are intentionally absent.

DiscoveryQueueDropPolicy.description: Discovery queue overflow behavior for unauthenticated input.

oneOf.description: Fail closed by dropping the new unauthenticated item when full.

DiscoveryQueuePolicy.description: Bounded policy for the pre-auth discovery/admission queue.

maxUnverifiedPeers.description: Maximum simultaneously tracked unverified peer handles.

EnrollmentReason.description: Stable rejection/supersession reason for enrollment metadata.

EnrollmentRecord.description: Metadata-only enrollment record for a child realm.

controllerGeneration.description: Active controller generation for this enrollment.

correlationId.description: Correlation id for audit across parent/child records.

parentRealm.description: Parent realm that admits the child.

ErrorKind.description: Stable, closed-enum classification of a realm operation failure. Codecs map this to/from typed error frames; it never carries payload bytes.

oneOf.description: A required capability was not advertised; fail closed (no fallback).
The principal is not authorized for this operation/stream.
No realm entrypoint matched the target.
The provider could not allocate/start the workload.
Relay reachability or relay authentication failed.
Same idempotency key, request still in progress.
Same idempotency key, different request fingerprint.
Idempotency key reused after its dedup retention window.
A bounded queue/credit window was exceeded.
A frame exceeded the negotiated cap before decode.
A malformed or unknown-shaped frame/field was rejected.
The target name could not be parsed.
Audit could not be recorded and policy is fail-closed.
The requested feature/transport mode is not implemented by this build (a typed, non-fallback refusal).
Attach to the interactive owner stream.

ExecAttachRequest.description: Attach/reconnect request for a durable execution.

ExecStartRequest.description: Durable execution start request metadata. Command details live in the opaque operation body and are never logged by this metadata model.

oneOf.description: Failed before a normal exit code was available.

ExecutionGeneration.description: Generation/boot binding for reconnect safety. A reconnect request must match the generation the durable execution was created under; otherwise it is stale and must fail closed rather than attach to a different boot.

guest_boot_id.description: Guest boot identifier or equivalent provider boot token.

ExecutionSummary.description: A durable execution summary. Argv/stdio/env are payload and never appear here; only bounded metadata.

generation.description: Generation this execution is bound to.

stderr_cursor.description: Last retained stderr cursor, if available.

stdout_cursor.description: Last retained stdout cursor, if available.

tty.description: Whether a TTY was allocated (metadata only).

GrantedHostResource.description: One resource granted under a lease.

Handshake.description: Negotiated wire/codec version and capability advertisement exchanged at the start of a peer session.

capabilities.description: Versioned positive capability assertions negotiated for this session.

codec_id.description: Codec id negotiated for this session (bounded token).

peer.description: Reserved peer-binding seam. Absent in the bootstrap protocol.

schema_fingerprint.description: Codec schema fingerprint selected for this session (bounded token).

HandshakeAccepted.description: Successful handshake outcome. Carries the exact version/codec/schema fingerprint selected for the session.

HandshakeRejected.description: Fail-closed handshake rejection. The reason is a closed enum so callers never parse an unbounded peer-provided string for authz behavior.

HandshakeRejectedReason.description: Closed reason a peer handshake was refused.

HostLocalPeerCredentialChecker.description: Component that verifies host-local peer credentials.

oneOf.description: The public daemon socket performs `SO_PEERCRED` admission.

HostLocalPeerCredentialSemantics.description: Evidence that a direct host-local binding preserves OS peer credentials.

HostLocalPeerCredentialSource.description: Source of peer credentials for a host-local direct socket.

oneOf.description: The connecting client process is the peer observed by `d2bd`.

HostLocalProxyStatus.description: Whether a host-local result uses a byte proxy.

oneOf.description: No proxy process is inserted between the client and `d2bd`.

HostResourceKind.description: Closed host-resource kinds the local root allocator can arbitrate.

IdentityAuditEventMetadata.description: Metadata attached to audit records for enrollment/rotation/revocation and recovery. It names only ids, realm paths, statuses, and reasons.

controllerGeneration.description: Controller generation that produced or accepted this pin.

peer.description: Parent or child label at the trust edge.

role.description: Key role represented by this fingerprint.

KeyRotationPlan.description: Metadata-only rotation plan. Replacement fields are opaque refs and fingerprints only; no generated key bytes or signatures are represented.

KeyRotationReason.description: Stable reason for a key rotation.

KeyRotationStatus.description: Lifecycle status for a key rotation plan.

KeyRotationSubjectKind.description: Key material class controlled by a rotation.

LeaseAllocationRequest.description: Mutating lease allocation request. Idempotency is mandatory.

idempotency_key.description: Caller-generated dedup key for at-least-once delivery.

LeaseAllocationResponse.description: Allocator response to a typed lease request.

LeaseAllocationResult.description: Granted or fail-closed denied allocation result.

LeaseOwner.description: The realm-controller generation that owns a lease.

controller_generation.description: Active controller generation that requested the lease.

node.description: Realm node/broker identity, when the caller has one bound.

LeaseResourceRequest.description: One resource requested from the local-root allocator.

LegacySurface.description: Legacy surface that triggered a realm-native migration diagnostic.

MigrationErrorEnvelope.description: Fail-closed migration diagnostic for removed gateway/ACA/group/env surfaces.

legacyId.description: Optional bounded legacy option/state identifier; never a path or secret.

NodeKind.description: What kind of node this is, and therefore what d2b can own.

oneOf.description: A full d2b host (KVM, broker, vsock, device control).

NodeSummary.description: A node's advertised summary. Capabilities are positive assertions.

realm.description: Realm this node belongs to. Inventory is realm-scoped; this is not a host-global registry key.

ObservedHostResource.description: Host-resource observation from the live system side of reconciliation.

ObservedResourceState.description: Kernel/host-observed state for one resource id.

OperationKind.description: The kind of a realm operation. Closed enum; unknown kinds are rejected at decode (fail-closed).

OperationRequest.description:
An operation request envelope. The operation-specific body is an opaque, bounded payload that a higher layer encodes; the routing/authz layer reasons over the typed envelope fields only.

The required capability is NOT a wire field — it is derived from [`OperationKind::required_capability`] in trusted code so a peer cannot downgrade it. The authenticated session principal MUST match [`OperationRequest::principal`]; the router rejects a mismatch.

correlation_id.description: Cross-realm correlation id shared across route and audit hops.

idempotency_key.description: Caller-generated idempotency key (required for mutating ops).

realm.description: Target realm path (most-specific-first; supports nested realms).

correlation_id.description: Cross-realm correlation id shared across response, route, and audit hops.

ParentTrustAnchor.description: Parent trust-anchor metadata installed into a child realm.

PeerContext.description: A reserved, non-secret peer-binding context for the session handshake. The bootstrap surface carries only the seam: later work populates the peer identity + transcript metadata so mutual auth can be added without a breaking wire change. It never carries secret key material.

auth_mechanism.description: Stable, non-secret auth-mechanism id negotiated for the session (e.g. `none` initially; `mtls`/`relay-sas-bound` later). Bounded.

peer_node.description: The peer's node id, once bound.

peer_principal.description: The peer's authenticated principal, once a mechanism binds one.

PersistedResourceLease.description: Persisted resource lease metadata from the allocator ledger.

ProviderRegistryEntry.description: Provider registry entry owned by a realm controller. Capabilities are positive assertions; absent capabilities must be treated as denial.

controllerPlacement.description: Controller placement through which the provider is managed.

realm.description: Realm that owns this registry row.

RealmAccessAliasBinding.description: One alias-table binding supplied as resolver input.

RealmAccessAliasSource.description: How a resolver learned about a target alias.

oneOf.description: The operator supplied a fully-qualified canonical target.
The alias came from a local alias table.

alias.description: Bare alias that matched the target.

oneOf.description: The resolver appended a selected default realm to a bare workload.

RealmAccessBinding.description: Resolved access binding for a realm controller generation.

controllerGeneration.description: Controller generation that issued this binding.

RealmAccessClientBinding.description: Client-consumable binding selected by the access resolver. The host-local variant is explicitly direct; a byte-proxy implementation would require a different, incompatible variant.

RealmAccessClientBindingKind.description: Client-supported access binding kinds. This is a contract declaration, not a transport implementation.

oneOf.description: Client can connect directly to a host-local Unix socket and preserve kernel `SO_PEERCRED` semantics.
Client can use a remote realm transport reference.
Client can use a provider transport reference.

RealmAccessClientContract.description: Client-side access contract presented to the resolver.

requireDirectLocalSoPeercred.description: When true, a host-local result must be a direct socket connection; a byte proxy is not acceptable because it would hide the original peer credentials from `d2bd`.

RealmAccessConflictCandidate.description: Candidate involved in alias ambiguity or binding conflict diagnostics.

RealmAccessResolverDiagnostic.description: Typed resolver diagnostics. These are safe to return to clients and audit: they carry bounded ids, targets, capabilities, and generation ids only.

RealmAccessResolverRequest.description: Input model for realm access resolution. Runtime routing can continue using existing paths while callers and tests exchange this contract shape.

RealmAccessResolverResponse.description: Successful resolver output for a canonical target and binding.

RealmControllerPlacement.description:
Where a realm controller is placed in the realm-native model.

This is capability-neutral placement metadata. Whether a placement can perform lifecycle, exec, display, or provider operations is advertised separately through [`crate::capability::CapabilitySet`].

oneOf.description: Isolated realm service on the physical host.

provider.description: Provider that owns the controller substrate.

oneOf.description: Agent inside or adjacent to a managed provider sandbox.

provider.description: Provider that owns the sandbox substrate.

oneOf.description: Provider-specific placement that is still described through the shared realm protocol and positive capability advertisement.

provider.description: Provider that defines the placement token.

RealmIdentityMetadata.description: Metadata for a realm identity key. The reference and fingerprint are the only key-shaped values; no key bytes or credential material are modeled.

RealmIdentityStatus.description: Lifecycle state of a realm identity reference.

RealmKeyRole.description: Role of a pinned realm key.

oneOf.description: Parent realm trust anchor pinned into a child.
Child realm identity observed and pinned by a parent.

RealmTransportBinding.description: Transport binding for reaching a realm controller.

oneOf.description: Direct host-local Unix socket. Authentication remains OS DAC + `SO_PEERCRED`; no local-root byte proxy is implied.

socket_path.description: Socket path for this realm controller.

oneOf.description: Remote realm-tree transport selected by the resolver.

binding_ref.description: Non-secret resolver reference for endpoint lookup.

oneOf.description: Provider-backed realm transport selected by the resolver.

ReconciliationDecision.description: Reconciliation decision for a persisted/live observation pair.

ReconciliationRecord.description: One reconciliation record distinguishes persisted lease state from live kernel/host-observed state.

RecoveryProcedure.description: Metadata-only recovery procedure for a lost or compromised child controller key. Evidence values are bounded opaque refs, not logs or secret bytes.

RecoveryStatus.description: Metadata lifecycle status for child-controller recovery.

ReplayWindowMetadata.description: Replay-window metadata for discovery/session/ad route deduplication.

ResourceAcquisitionKey.description: Deterministic total-order key for resource acquisition.

ResourceAcquisitionOrder.description: Explicit acquisition order used for multi-resource requests.

ordinal.description: Order within the phase. Lower ordinals are acquired first.

phase.description: Coarse phase. Lower phases are acquired first.

ResourceDelegation.description: How a granted resource is delegated to a realm broker.

oneOf.description: Delegated opaque name/handle. It is not a raw interface/path name.
FD handoff placeholder id used only for correlation before SCM_RIGHTS.

ResourceObservationSource.description: Contract-only source for observed host state. These values describe which subsystem produced an observation; they do not imply an implementation here.

oneOf.description: No other owner may hold the same resource id.
The allocator grants a partition inside a shared resource.

RevocationTarget.description: Thing revoked by a parent or realm controller.

controllerGeneration.description: Controller generation that signed the advertisement.

expiresAtUnixSeconds.description: Expiry time as Unix seconds. Must be greater than issue time.

treeEdge.description: Parent/child edge that authorizes this advertisement.

RouteAdvertisementEnvelope.description: Envelope around a signed route advertisement as seen by the receiving realm.

admission.description: Bounded receipt/admission metadata, not raw relay bytes.

correlationId.description: Bounded correlation id shared across route-decision audit records.

replayWindow.description: Replay window used to reject duplicate adverts.

trace.description: Optional bounded trace context carried alongside the advert.

RouteAuditEventKind.description: Route event kind for bounded audit metadata.

RouteAuditLabels.description: Route audit labels. These may include a bounded policy id for operator audit, but never payloads, relay endpoints, peer identity strings, or raw metrics dimensions.

RouteFailClosedReason.description: Closed fail-closed route/admission reason. These labels are safe for audit and low-cardinality counters.

RouteNamespaceAllocation.description: Namespace delegated by a parent to a direct child for route advertisements.

allowedPrefixes.description: Prefixes the child may advertise. Each prefix must be the child realm or a descendant under it; siblings and parents are rejected.

RoutePlacementClass.description: Low-cardinality placement class. Provider ids and raw transport addresses stay out of telemetry labels.

RouteRealmClass.description: Low-cardinality realm class suitable for route metrics.

RouteSignature.description: Signature metadata for a route advertisement.

keyRole.description: Controller key role used for signing.

signatureRef.description: Detached signature reference or bounded signature fingerprint.

signingKeyFingerprint.description: Fingerprint of the signing key; no key bytes.

RouteTelemetryLabels.description: Low-cardinality metric labels for route telemetry. It intentionally carries no realm path, target address, raw transport address, or peer identifier.

RouteTelemetrySample.description: One bounded route telemetry counter sample.

SessionTeardownDirective.description: Metadata-only directive to terminate sessions depending on a revoked grant.

SessionTeardownReason.description: Runtime session teardown cause metadata. This only describes what must be torn down; it does not implement routing, transport, or process cleanup.

ShellAttachSummary.description: Successful `ShellAttach` metadata. The terminal stream itself is opened as `StreamKind::ShellPty`, so raw terminal bytes stay out of this metadata.

ShellCause.description: Bounded cause for an observed shell state transition.

ShellEventBatch.description: Response body for a shell lifecycle event poll. `reconciliation_gap` tells callers that older events were dropped before `cursor`; callers must re-list shells before presenting state as complete.

ShellEventSummary.description: Bounded realm-safe shell lifecycle event metadata. It never carries terminal bytes, argv, environment, cwd, provider endpoints, or credentials.

ShellGeneration.description: Boot/daemon-generation metadata used to reject stale shell operations.

ShellSummary.description: Bounded summary returned by `ShellList` and status-like responses.

StreamAuthz.description: The authorization context evaluated before a stream is opened. The `capability` is always derived from the stream kind via [`StreamAuthz::for_kind`] so a caller cannot pair a stream kind with a weaker capability.

capability.description: Capability required for the stream kind (derived from the kind).

realm.description: Realm the stream belongs to (full path; supports nested realms).

StreamChannel.description:
The logical sub-channel a [`crate::frame::StreamData`] chunk belongs to.

Most stream kinds carry a single `Primary` channel. A `Stdio` stream in non-TTY mode multiplexes `Stdout` and `Stderr` over one stream so the two are ordered against each other yet still distinguishable — a peer MUST NOT have to open two streams (and two authz contexts) just to split stdout from stderr. The channel is a closed enum; an unknown channel is rejected at decode (fail-closed).

oneOf.description: The default single channel for a stream kind.

StreamCloseReason.description: Why a stream was closed. Carried on [`crate::frame::StreamClose`] so the peer can distinguish an orderly end-of-stream from a cancellation, timeout, or error without inspecting payload bytes. Closed enum; an unknown reason is rejected at decode (fail-closed).

oneOf.description: Orderly end-of-stream: all data was delivered.
The stream was cancelled by a peer (e.g. `ExecCancel`).
The stream exceeded its inactivity/operation deadline.
The stream ended because of an error (details travel in a separate typed-error frame, never in this reason).
The remote end of the stream went away (process/relay/gateway gone).

StreamDescriptor.description: A request to open a named stream.

id.description: Stream id within the peer session.

StreamKind.description: The kind of a named stream. Each kind maps to a required capability.

oneOf.description: Persistent-shell terminal bytes authorized by a ShellAttach operation.
Durable execution logs with resume cursors.
Bounded chunks, hashes, explicit destination policy.
One stream per connection; never a generic network bridge.
Audio capture (workload → host); a distinct capability from playback.
Named USB device operations; a distinct capability from HID.

StreamResume.description: Reattach/resume request for a durable stream. The cursor is meaningful only for resumable stream kinds such as logs; the mux rejects resumes for non-resumable streams before any transport replay can occur.

cursor.description: Durable cursor the peer wants to resume from.

TreeRouteDecision.description: Pure metadata route decision. It carries no transport sockets or relay endpoints.

TreeRouteDecisionOutcome.description: Result of a tree route decision.

TreeRouteHop.description: One validated parent/child hop in a tree route.

TreeRouteHopDirection.description: Direction for one hop along the realm tree.

TreeRoutePath.description: Bounded tree path used for a route decision.

UnverifiedPeerAdmissionAttemptMetadata.description: Metadata for an unauthenticated peer discovery/admission attempt.

claimedRealm.description: Claimed realm, if the pre-auth message carried a parseable realm path.

ingressClass.description: Coarse ingress class, never a raw relay endpoint.

queueDepth.description: Current queue depth after applying the admission decision.

trace.description: Optional bounded trace context propagated by the caller.

unverifiedPeerRef.description: Redacted queue-local peer handle, never a metric label.

WorkloadPlacement.description: Coarse workload placement kind for inventory and preflight output.

oneOf.description: Workload runs on a host-local runtime provider.

node.description: Node that currently owns the workload.

oneOf.description: Workload runs inside a gateway VM realm.

gateway.description: Gateway node that currently owns the workload.

oneOf.description: Workload runs on a cloud full-host realm.

WorkloadPlacementSummary.description: Workload placement summary for machine-readable inventory.

realm.description: Realm this workload belongs to. Inventory is realm-scoped; this does not imply a host-global workload registry.
